Adam (Pre-Fall).The Fall: Created morally neutral. His fall only provided a bad example; it did not corrupt human nature.
Adam (Pre-Fall).The Cross: Not an atoning sacrifice for original sin, but the ultimate moral example of perfect obedience.
Adam (Pre-Fall).Conversion: N/A - Adam was created with the capacity to live perfectly.
Adam (Pre-Fall).Outward Deeds: Fully capable of choosing perfect good by natural free will.
Adam (Pre-Fall).Heart Motive: Capable of pure motives without needing internal grace.

Jesus Christ.The Fall: Unaffected; came to reverse Adam's bad example with a perfect example.
Jesus Christ.The Cross: A demonstration of perfect love and obedience for humanity to imitate.
Jesus Christ.Conversion: N/A - Lived a perfectly sinless life.

Unregenerate Man.The Fall: Born exactly as Adam was before the fall: a blank slate with total free will. No original sin.
Unregenerate Man.The Cross: Beneficiary only in the sense of having a supreme moral example to follow.
Unregenerate Man.Conversion: Fully capable of choosing God and converting by their own natural free will. Grace is merely helpful instruction.
Unregenerate Man.Outward Deeds: Can achieve sinless perfection strictly through natural willpower.
Unregenerate Man.Heart Motive: Capable of generating pure love for God without supernatural intervention.

Regenerate Man.The Fall: Saved by successfully imitating Christ rather than imitating Adam.
Regenerate Man.The Cross: Looks to the cross as the ultimate guide for moral living.
Regenerate Man.Conversion: Converted by making a rational, unaided choice to obey God's law.
Regenerate Man.Outward Deeds: Follows the law and Christ's example.
Regenerate Man.Heart Motive: Chooses to act purely out of natural capacity.

Adam (Pre-Fall).The Fall: Created good, but his fall severely injured and weakened human nature.
Adam (Pre-Fall).Outward Deeds: Able to choose perfect good before the fall.
Adam (Pre-Fall).Heart Motive: Able to act purely for God before the fall.

Jesus Christ.The Fall: The unfallen Savior who brings necessary grace to a wounded humanity.
Jesus Christ.The Cross: Died to provide the grace necessary to complete human salvation.

Unregenerate Man.The Fall: Inherits a sick and deeply wounded nature, but the will is not totally dead.
Unregenerate Man.The Cross: Needs the cross to be saved, but must take the first step toward it.
Unregenerate Man.Conversion: Retains a tiny sliver of natural free will. Man makes the first move to seek God, and God responds with grace.
Unregenerate Man.Outward Deeds: Can perform some civil good, but needs grace to complete it.
Unregenerate Man.Heart Motive: Cannot purify the heart entirely, but can naturally desire to be healed.

Regenerate Man.The Fall: Healed from the sickness of the fall through cooperative grace.
Regenerate Man.The Cross: Saved by grace, which they actively cooperated with.
Regenerate Man.Conversion: Synergistically partnered with God; grace was necessary, but human choice was the decisive factor.
Regenerate Man.Outward Deeds: Cooperates with grace to live a moral life.
Regenerate Man.Heart Motive: Desires God, sustained by a partnership of grace and free will.

Adam (Pre-Fall).The Fall: Created with true free will; freely chose to rebel.
Adam (Pre-Fall).The Cross: His rebellion made the atonement necessary.

Jesus Christ.The Fall: The Second Adam who provides a remedy for total depravity.
Jesus Christ.The Cross: Died for all humanity (Universal Atonement) to make salvation possible for everyone.

Unregenerate Man.The Fall: Inherits total depravity; naturally spiritually dead and completely bound to sin.
Unregenerate Man.The Cross: Christ died for them, but the benefit is not applied until they believe.
Unregenerate Man.Conversion: Naturally incapable, BUT God gives 'prevenient grace' to all, supernaturally restoring free will so they can choose to accept or reject the Gospel.
Unregenerate Man.Outward Deeds: Capable of civil good, but only by common grace.
Unregenerate Man.Heart Motive: Cannot genuinely seek God without the universal gift of prevenient grace.

Regenerate Man.The Fall: Redeemed by freely accepting God's offered grace.
Regenerate Man.The Cross: Beneficiary of the atonement through the instrument of free faith.
Regenerate Man.Conversion: Freely cooperated with God's grace; their choice was the deciding factor. They can potentially fall away (apostasy).
Regenerate Man.Outward Deeds: Empowered by the Spirit to live holy lives.
Regenerate Man.Heart Motive: Empowered to love God truly, though perfection is debated.

Adam (Pre-Fall).The Fall: Acted as federal head; possessed free will but voluntarily chose rebellion.
Adam (Pre-Fall).The Cross: His rebellion made the Cross necessary.
Adam (Pre-Fall).Heart Motive: Able to act purely for God's glory.

Jesus Christ.The Cross: Died specifically to secure the salvation of the elect (Definite/Limited Atonement).
Jesus Christ.Heart Motive: Perfectly aligned with the Father's will.

Unregenerate Man.The Fall: Guilty by imputation; totally depraved and spiritually dead.
Unregenerate Man.The Cross: Acted wickedly (e.g., Judas), fulfilling God's sovereign plan voluntarily.
Unregenerate Man.Conversion: Utterly incapable. Requires irresistible, monergistic grace to resurrect their dead heart so they can believe.
Unregenerate Man.Outward Deeds: Capable of civil righteousness (e.g., charity) in the horizontal realm.
Unregenerate Man.Heart Motive: Incapable of pleasing God; all deeds tainted by the sin of self-justification.

Regenerate Man.The Fall: Redeemed from guilt, though the flesh remains to be battled.
Regenerate Man.The Cross: The guaranteed beneficiary of effectual atonement.
Regenerate Man.Conversion: Willingly converted ONLY because God first changed their nature.
Regenerate Man.Outward Deeds: Freed to do genuinely good works.
Regenerate Man.Heart Motive: Able to act out of genuine love for God, free from the need to earn salvation.

Adam (Pre-Fall).The Fall: A theological saga/myth representing the universal human reality of rejecting God's grace.
Adam (Pre-Fall).The Cross: N/A - Adam represents the human condition that the Cross addresses.
Adam (Pre-Fall).Outward Deeds: Represents humanity's attempt to live autonomously.

Jesus Christ.The Fall: The true center of history; humanity is defined by Christ, not Adam.
Jesus Christ.The Cross: Christ is both the Electing God and the only truly Reprobate (damned) Man, taking all rejection upon Himself.
Jesus Christ.Conversion: The objective reality of all human salvation is found exclusively in Him.

Unregenerate Man.The Fall: Lives in the impossible illusion of being separated from God.
Unregenerate Man.The Cross: Already objectively reconciled to God at the Cross, but does not know it yet.
Unregenerate Man.Conversion: Not a change of eternal status, but the subjective awakening to the reality that they are already elect in Christ.
Unregenerate Man.Outward Deeds: Can do moral good, but lives in an ontological lie.
Unregenerate Man.Heart Motive: Blind to the grace that already defines their existence.

Regenerate Man.The Fall: Freed from the illusion of the Fall.
Regenerate Man.The Cross: Lives in joyful gratitude for the objective triumph of the Cross.
Regenerate Man.Conversion: Has had their eyes opened to the reality of their election in Christ.
Regenerate Man.Outward Deeds: Actions reflect gratitude for accomplished grace.
Regenerate Man.Heart Motive: Motivated by the joyous knowledge of God's 'Yes' to humanity.

Adam (Pre-Fall).The Fall: Represents humanity trapped in self-estrangement and the status quo of the past.
Adam (Pre-Fall).Heart Motive: Represents a heart closed off to God's future.

Jesus Christ.The Fall: The one who breaks the cycle of history and opens the future.
Jesus Christ.The Cross: The Crucified God; God takes godforsakenness and suffering into His own being in absolute solidarity with the oppressed.

Unregenerate Man.The Fall: Trapped in the 'hell of self-estrangement' and closed off to the future.
Unregenerate Man.The Cross: The object of God's solidarity in their suffering and oppression.
Unregenerate Man.Conversion: Paralyzed not just by moral failure, but by hopelessness, apathy, and resignation to the world's suffering.
Unregenerate Man.Outward Deeds: Accepts the unjust status quo of the world.
Unregenerate Man.Heart Motive: Suffers from the sin of despair and lack of hope.

Regenerate Man.The Fall: Liberated from the past to become a participant in God's coming future.
Regenerate Man.The Cross: Finds freedom in the resurrection breaking into the present reality.
Regenerate Man.Conversion: Liberated by eschatological hope to stop accepting suffering and start transforming the world.
Regenerate Man.Outward Deeds: Active in ministering, suffering with others, and fighting for systemic justice.
Regenerate Man.Heart Motive: Driven by the hope of the coming Kingdom of God.
